content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the feature diff across run history, task detail lineage, and milestone lineage query code, and read the key implementation files end-to-end. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --check` passed locally. I found 2 fix-now robustness issues.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref` now treats every all-numeric dotted bead ref as a local short alias, which breaks matching when milestone IDs themselves are numeric.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:8-23`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:4371-4372`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:4997-4998`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:5180-5183`
remediation_hint: Do not blindly prefix every numeric dotted ref. Either prove the dotted alias belongs to the current milestone (for example from the current plan/bead graph) before canonicalizing, or narrow the heuristic so fully qualified foreign numeric milestone refs are preserved.
   Rationale: `MilestoneId::new` accepts purely numeric identifiers (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:24-31`). With the new helper, milestone `10` will reinterpret foreign bead `8.5.3` as local `10.8.5.3`. The FS lineage store uses this matcher for active-run detection and bead-history filtering, so the wrong attempt can now be merged, blocked, or returned.
2. finding_summary: The new lineage queries silently swallow every `load_plan_bundle` failure and return fallback lineage instead of surfacing unexpected plan-store errors.
severity: low
affected_files:
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2247-2250`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2286-2288`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2352-2353`
remediation_hint: Keep the current fallback for expected stale-plan cases, but propagate unrelated `AppError`s such as filesystem/permission failures so callers can distinguish "plan unavailable/corrupt" from "the store is broken".
   Rationale: `read_bead_lineage`, `bead_execution_history`, and `list_tasks_for_milestone` currently turn all `load_plan_bundle` errors into partial success. If `plan.json` becomes unreadable for an operational reason, CLI/controller queries will quietly drop bead titles and acceptance criteria, which makes trace data look valid while hiding the underlying storage failure.
